mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce discount rates for future corporate earnings, directly increasing present value of S&P 500 cash flows and generating positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in >60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or out-of-sample hit rate falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses statistical significance (p>0.1) in multivariate regression over 5-year sample, or joint signal yields negative IC.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index reflects accelerating manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum through improved earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during inventory buildup periods, yielding negative IC over 1-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 230.0 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures, prompting expectations of aggressive central bank tightening that compresses equity valuations.
failure_condition: Market prices in policy response immediately, resulting in flat or positive forward returns despite high CPI readings.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, boosting consumer spending capacity and generating positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: Correlation between PAYEMS and UNRATE breaks down, rendering the interaction term statistically insignificant and predictive power null.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP directly translates to higher aggregate corporate revenues and profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: GDP revisions erase initial growth signal, causing forward returns to underperform baseline by >200 bps across multiple quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index falling below 95.0 signals severe manufacturing contraction and supply-side bottlenecks, triggering immediate risk aversion and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >65% of instances when INDPRO crosses below 95.0, invalidating the contraction signal.
